method: COMMENT_EVALUATION batch 4 — A6 gating pointers (researcher-approved 2026-06-02). 24 SD_POINTER gating flags on M38's registries. 19 are mercy (reg 111) inter-characteristic content pointers -> moved to M05 (SD_POINTER->SD_CLUSTER, cluster_link=M05). 5 are reg 180/187 pure process / database / registry-design pointers (Session D moot) -> resolved + closed (notes preserve the real sha.ma data issue). Clears M38 A6 -> 0.

resolve_flags.resolved_note: Resolved as registry-design pointer (Session D moot). The yielding-registry-coherence question ('is yielding coherent enough for a single registry?') is superseded by the term-anchor re-clustering — reg 180 is no longer an analytical unit; its terms are distributed by characteristic (yielding/paradidōmi homed to M44). Closed.

Resolved as database/process pointer (Session D moot). The sha.ma (H8085H) STEP sub-gloss extraction gap is a REAL data issue — it is carried forward via the M41 mis-clustering flag (flag 720, comment 158 routed to M30): when M30/M41 are analysed, reassess sha.ma/qashav extraction + placement. Closed here as the registry-process pointer; the data issue is not lost.

Resolved as registry-design pointer (Session D moot). The oikos/oikia 'metaphorical register' question is a registry-structure musing superseded by term-anchor re-clustering (household terms distributed by characteristic). Closed.

Resolved as cross-registry synthesis pointer (Session D moot). The chazaq/amats courage-formula (chazaq ve-amats) C08/C20 split synthesis will be handled when M23 (Strength) analyses the chazaq/amats terms. Closed as a registry-process pointer.

Resolved as methodology pointer (Session D moot). The directionally-determined principle (same root in opposite registers) is an established cross-cluster methodology note, not a cluster finding. Closed.
